--- SOURCE 5: NANOGrav signal from axion inflation | Phys. Rev. D ---
URL: https://link.aps.org/doi/10.1103/PhysRevD.108.115023

SUMMARY:
by X Niu · 2023 · Cited by 55 — We report that such a signal can arise from the Chern-Simons coupling in axion inflation, where a pseudoscalar inflaton couples to a (massive) U ⁡ ( 1 ) gauge



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://astrobites.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/noaj-gw.jpeg
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates the evolution of the universe from its birth, through primordial gravitational waves and the cosmic microwave background, to current observations, highlighting insights into cosmic inflation and gravitational waves.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://nanograv.github.io/11yr_stochastic_analysis/images/cosmicstrings.png
DESCRIPTION: The image shows a logarithmic plot displaying the relationship between power (p) and the gravitational wave strain amplitude parameter (Gμ/c²), with shaded regions and data points indicating the constraints from NanoGrav 11-year, NANOGrav 9-year, and EPTA 2015 observations related to cosmic inflation insights.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://ai2-s2-public.s3.amazonaws.com/figures/2017-08-08/d7d4e40e3894e5ffa1a7709942597ca3d6dab7ae/5-Figure3-1.png
DESCRIPTION: The graph displays the statistical analysis of signals related to cosmic inflation, highlighting the resonance behavior of the stochastic gravitational wave background as observed by NANOGrav.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://nanograv.org/sites/default/files/styles/16_x_9_teaser/public/2023-06/GravWaves_2560_HQ.jpg?itok=nTNl5IP1
DESCRIPTION: Swirling cosmic waves and black holes are depicted over a grid with pulsar-like objects, illustrating concepts related to gravitational waves and cosmic inflation.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:1200/1*UCI_HquyXzSTshjpx6CzUg.png
DESCRIPTION: Vivid golden and blue swirling patterns emanate from a bright central point, resembling cosmic waves or gravitational phenomena linked to cosmic inflation and gravitational wave research.

enriched_points.expanded_content: The NANOGrav collaboration's recent detection of a common-spectrum gravitational wave background suggests potential cosmic origins, enhancing our understanding of astrophysical phenomena.
NANOGrav's results may provide clues about cosmic inflation, a rapid expansion phase post-Big Bang, potentially revealing mechanisms behind the universe's large-scale structure.
This research aids in reconstructing early universe conditions, linking gravitational waves to inflationary models that describe the formation of cosmic structures.

enriched_points.point_title: Link to dark matter and energy
enriched_points.expanded_content: The findings could also illuminate the nature of dark matter and energy, possibly connecting gravitational waves with elusive cosmic constituents shaping the universe.
Future investigations will focus on refining gravitational wave detection techniques, exploring their implications on cosmology, and testing theories about the universe's inception.
